name: Andrea's Fudge Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and place the rack in the center.
2. Lightly butter a 9-inch springform pan and dust it with flour, tapping out any excess.
3. In the top of a double boiler over hot, not simmering water, melt espresso or coffee and chopped chocolate together, stirring occasionally until cooled to room temperature.
4. In a large bowl, cream sugar and butter together until light and fluffy.
5. Add egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in the flour.
6. In a grease-free bowl, beat egg whites with a mixer on medium-high speed until they form stiff, shiny peaks.
7. Fold 1/4 of the whites into the chocolate mixture to lighten it.
8. Continue folding in the remaining whites and butter-flour mixture.
9. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 60-70 minutes or until the top is crusty and cracked and the center is still slightly moist.
10. Remove cake from oven and let cool completely on a wire rack.
11. Carefully remove the sides of the springform pan and transfer the cake to a serving plate. Optionally, place a doily on top and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar before serving.
